item.title: 'They'll walk away': Home-sellers change tack as auction clearance rates plunge
item.description: Auctions aren't completely disappearing, but softer conditions are changing when some sellers decide to make a deal.

item.title: Support for multiculturalism is declining. Why is that so?
item.description: A new report by the Lowy Institute reveals support for multiculturalism in Australia has plunged in the past two years. A recent poll has linked a decline in support for both current migration levels and multiculturalism, which coincides with growing fears about economic and social safety.

item.title: An egg shortage? Human infection? The ways H5 bird flu could impact Australia
item.description: The H5 bird flu variant can infect poultry, domestic cats, dairy cattle and a range of wildlife.

item.title: Has Trump and Netanyahu's push for regime change in Iran backfired?
item.description: Far from abolishing or weakening it, some believe the US-Israeli war has left Iran's regime emboldened and strengthened.

item.title: $400k, $630k or $1m? How to know which super number matters to you
item.description: There are a lot of retirement figures and estimates floating around. What should you actually aim for?

item.title: 'Desperate' IVF patients offered treatments with no proven fertility benefits
item.description: Many IVF patients would pay for costly extra treatments to increase chances of pregnancy, but new research find many aren't effective.

item.title: South Australia has confirmed its first case of H5 bird flu as outbreak crosses state lines
item.description: CSIRO testing has confirmed that a migratory seabird found on the Fleurieu Peninsula had the deadly disease.

item.title: Inflation cools but rate rise risks remain
item.description: Australia's inflation rate eased in May, offering some relief to households, but sticky underlying price pressures suggest the Reserve Bank may not be finished with interest rates just yet

item.title: What you won't get this tax time — and the claims the ATO will be watching
item.description: Australian taxpayers will have to wait until next year for a proposed deduction to come into effect.

item.title: France records hottest day on record as Europe swelters through brutal heatwave
item.description: An estimated 44 million people in France are currently under the highest red alert level for heat.

item.title: Third bird flu result recorded
Trump says talks are going well
UN slams Australia over offshore detention
Venezuela earthquake death toll rises to 188, more than 1,500 injured
item.description: The strongest quake to hit Venezuela in 126 years will require "massive collective efforts", the UN aid chief said.

item.title: The World Cup's breakout stars aren't who you'd expect
item.description: Some of the most memorable World Cup moments are coming from off the pitch.

item.title: Earthquakes Hit Venezuela | SBS News in Easy English 25 June 2026
item.description: A news bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Everything you need to know about Labor's latest tax changes
item.description: The sweeping changes to Australia's economy passed federal parliament today, following a deal between Labor and the Greens.

item.title: Unemployment rate falls as oil, gold and Australian dollar hit milestones
item.description: Australia's unemployment rate unexpectedly eased in May despite a cooling labour market, but what does it mean for the Reserve Bank after inflation also ticked higher? Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Indeed APAC Senior Economist Callam Pickering about the outlook for interest rates, before LGT Wealth Management Chief Investment Officer Scott Haslem explains why investors moved into defensive stocks as the Australian dollar slipped below 69 US cents, oil fell below pre-conflict levels and gold dropped under US$4,000.

item.title: Are buyers like Mehdi the winners of Australia’s housing downturn?
item.description: Australia’s housing market is shifting, creating new opportunities for some and fresh uncertainty for others.

item.title: Sickies, long lunches, green-and-gold fever: Australia set for a midday Socceroos party
item.description: With the Socceroos one match away from the World Cup knockout rounds, employers are being urged to embrace football fever.

item.title: The 'yellow line' Gazans have been killed for crossing, according to new report
item.description: Israel rejected the UN's independent inquiry, which found children in Gaza are still targeted after the ceasefire.

item.title: Trump touts progress with Iran, Starmer resigns & the far-right on the rise in Australia
item.description: Trump and JD Vance insist talks for a final deal between the US and Iran are progressing smoothly, but does Iran agree? Keir Starmer bows to party pressure and resigns as PM, what's next for the UK? Plus, SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson talks One Nation's rise up the polls in Australia, and her run-in with leader Pauline Hanson. And, it's fans, fun and fishy business during this week's action at the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English | 26 June 2026
item.description: A news bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Tech sell-off rocks Asia as investors contemplate outlook
item.description: The Australian sharemarket finished higher despite a volatile session and a global technology sell-off that triggered a second circuit breaker in South Korea this week. Apple price hikes and concerns over AI valuations weighed on tech stocks, while gold miners found support and the ASX closed the week down 0.9 per cent. Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Tom Wickenden from Betashares about what drove today's market moves and what investors should watch next.

item.title: The night sky is fading. What happens when we lose the stars?
item.description: As the night sky grows brighter, some Australians are fighting to save what's disappearing from above them.

item.title: Chinese food in Australia is changing – thanks to a new generation of chefs
item.description: Across Australia, rising costs and staff shortages have forced many traditional Chinese restaurants to close. Some younger chefs are forging a new path with fusion styles that celebrate local ingredients.

item.title: How scientists are using surrogates to protect Australia's most endangered wallaby
item.description: Everyone from rugby union supporters to wildlife and animal lovers has heard of the Australian wallaby. But it's perhaps less known that there are, in fact, many species of these cute marsupials, including some that are endangered. One of these is the brush-tailed rock-wallaby and scientists are now working on ways to protect the species.

item.title: Iran strikes United States-linked targets, accuses US of 'blatant violation' of peace deal
item.description: The US military said it struck several Iranian sites on Friday after an Iranian attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Tax time nears, inflation eases — and property loses momentum
item.description: The key money stories this week, broken down simply — and what they mean for you.

item.title: Why Chinese restaurants are disappearing — and evolving
item.description: As costs rise and tastes shift, a new generation is redefining Chinese food in Australia.

item.title: Socceroos stop the nation as Australia v Paraguay smashes World Cup viewing records
item.description: Were you one of the 4.8 million who tuned in on Friday?

item.title: 'This is history': World Cup fairytale performance thrills small nation's local diaspora
item.description: The nation of just 500,000 people has defied the odds to reach the knockout rounds, delighting Cape Verdeans in Australia.

item.title: Fourth positive bird flu case confirmed amid surge of calls to animal disease hotline
item.description: More than four hundred reports have been made to the emergency animal hotline in just over a week.

item.title: 'Thinfluencers': For Luka, healthy eating became an obsession. Her mum blames content creators
item.description: A new generation of influencers is promoting extreme thinness to millions online — with often-tragic consequences.

item.title: 6.7 million people affected by Venezuela earthquakes, UN says, locals plead for more aid
item.description: The death toll for the disaster that struck the South American nation on Thursday has risen to more than 14,000.

item.title: Iran again asserts control over Strait of Hormuz after Trump's Truth Social annihilation threat
item.description: The Iranian foreign minister has asserted Iran's control of the strategic waterway after a dire warning from the US president.

item.title: Alex and Judy have been together for 30 years, but they've never shared a home
item.description: For some Australians who meet their partner later in life, the concept of a 'living apart together' relationship can be appealing.

item.title: Those in Australia feel the shift. Those outside are watching closely
item.description: One Nation's rise is hardening Australia's migration debate — shaping belonging for those inside and decisions for those outside.

item.title: Paul Hogan hits back at 'pelican' Hanson over 'monoculture' claim
item.description: The One Nation leader's approval ratings dropped after her National Press Club speech.

item.title: Some households can get free electricity from Wednesday. Are you eligible?
item.description: A new scheme will give some households three hours of free electricity each day — even if they don't have rooftop solar.

item.title: Renewed fighting tests fragile peace deal for US, Iran
item.description: Just two weeks after the United States and Iran agreed to halt four months of fighting, renewed military exchanges are testing the fragile ceasefire. As tensions rise around the Strait of Hormuz, violence continues in Lebanon and Gaza, raising fresh questions about the prospects for lasting peace across the Middle East.

item.title: Government warns against AI content | SBS News in Easy English 29 June 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: The $54 million sponsorships raising questions about influence over Aussie kids
item.description: Six children's programs had a $54 million backing from resource companies, with hundreds more deals, prompting calls for scrutiny.

item.title: Australians caught up in Venezuela quake as Gold Coast firefighter tells of survivor search
item.description: With no Spanish or local contacts, Craig De Meillon flew to Venezuela to help search for earthquake survivors.

item.title: 1300 heat-linked deaths: why experts say Europe is in danger when it comes to extreme heat
item.description: The World Health Organisation says more than 1300 deaths can now be linked to Europe's unprecedented summer heatwave. As the heat moves east, all-time temperature records have been broken in Germany, Poland and the Czech Republic. Experts say with global warming making heatwaves more frequent, severe, and deadly, Europe is particularly vulnerable when it comes to soaring temperatures.

item.title: Super Platforms Face ASIC Scrutiny as the ASX Rallies Into Financial Year End
item.description: The ASX 200 rallied 0.7 per cent in a late-session surge ahead of the final trading day of the financial year, with tech stocks leading the gains. Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with ASIC Commissioner Simone Constant about why the corporate watchdog says superannuation trustees must do more to protect members from high fees and risky investments on investment platforms. Plus, Kyle Rodda from Capital.com breaks down the day's market action and what investors can expect as the financial year draws to a close.

item.title: Australians are feeling the strain of financial pressure. Could 'frugal influencers' help?
item.description: The influencer business model has long been based on selling a luxury lifestyle. But these creators want to do the opposite.

item.title: China reacts to Australia’s deal with Vanuatu | SBS News in Easy English 30 June 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Could this be Australia’s warmest winter ever?
item.description: The ski season has started, but the weather might mean it isn't worthwhile hitting the slopes just yet.

item.title: ASX lags global markets this financial year but super delivers strong returns
item.description: The Australian sharemarket ended the financial year with a modest gain, trailing the strong performance of global markets, but many Australians are still set to see healthy returns in their super. SBS On the Money speaks with Kai Chen, Head of Investment at MPC Markets, about why the ASX underperformed overseas markets, and Jeff Brunton, Deputy Chief Investment Officer at HESTA, on why diversified super funds are expected to deliver solid returns and what APRA's latest report says about the growing influence of Australia's superannuation sector on the nation's financial stability.

item.title: The city defying Australia's housing slump — and why sellers are still reaping record gains
item.description: Despite fresh price falls, many homeowners are still selling well ahead.

item.title: Pay less, owe it longer: What the HELP shake-up means for young Australians
item.description: New HELP rules mean smaller repayments now — but a longer debt later. We crunch the numbers on who really comes out ahead.

item.title: Australia's supermarket price crackdown starts today. But will you pay less?
item.description: The new laws target excessive grocery prices, but experts say their impact at the checkout may be limited.

item.title: WATCH: Mexico dismantle Ecuador with pitch perfect play and a pair of beautiful goals
item.description: Meanwhile, Mbappé got a double for France, and Norway celebrated a historic win over Côte d'Ivoire.

item.title: Australia's happiness divide has been revealed — and one worry stands out
item.description: More than 15,000 Australians were asked about happiness, optimism and the nation’s biggest concerns. One issue dominated.

item.title: Fuel prices rise as discount ends | SBS News in Easy English 1 July 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability

item.title: More than 12,000km from Washington, Trump's major move is still being felt
item.description: One year on from the dismantling of the world's largest donor of humanitarian aid, the impacts are devastating.

item.title: How 120,000 siblings disproved a myth around pregnancy and paracetamol
item.description: A new study found paracetamol use in pregnant mothers does not increase the risk of autism or ADHD.

item.title: Home prices record biggest monthly fall in three years as shares start the new financial year weaker
item.description: The new financial year has begun with a weaker tone for investors, as the ASX 200 fell 0.6 per cent and Australia's housing market recorded its biggest monthly decline since June 2022. SBS On the Money examines what is driving the latest property data with Tim Lawless, Head of Research at Cotality, before unpacking the day's market moves, sector rotation and takeover speculation with Hebe Chen, Market Analyst at Vantage Australia.

item.title: Ninth life: What 30 years of housing downturns can tell us about this one
item.description: Yes, property prices are falling. Here's what happened the last eight times — and the signs worth watching now.

item.title: Who had it worse? The hidden shift behind Australia’s generational mortgage debate
item.description: New research suggests borrowers are under greater stress than ever — including 1989 when interest rates were 17.5 per cent.

item.title: Cecily always wanted to marry Terry. She had to wait until he turned 90
item.description: Terry, 90, and Cecily, 89, have been together nearly 40 years. They say they're very different, but love each other more as time goes on.

item.title: 'Damning admission': Labor concession on controversial aged care tool fails to quell anger
item.description: Critics say a tool designed to streamline aged care assessments is leaving some people waiting too long.

item.title: What's behind South Africa's deadly anti-immigrant protests?
item.description: Organisers claim they are protesting against undocumented migrants, but some are seeking to spread the blame for other issues

item.title: SBS News in Easy English | Fans in Mexico die after football match
item.description: A news bulletin for English language learners

item.title: A week after Venezuela's quakes, one missed call is keeping hope alive
item.description: Families are clinging to signs of life beneath the rubble as anger grows over Venezuela's earthquake response.

item.title: Venezuela twin quake catastrophe, not-so-Strait shooting hinders US-Iran deal & Cuba opens up
item.description: Grief and anger mount in Venezuela after two huge earthquakes. SBS Correspondent Catalina Florez joins us from Caracas, where locals say the government is "nowhere to be seen". Plus, US and Iranian strikes resume, as a battle of wills over the Strait of Hormuz threatens to undo the ceasefire. Meanwhile, Cuba pushes through its biggest economic reforms since the communist revolution, and Batman Returns?! The caped crusader spotted in the most unlikely of places.

item.title: Salah or the Socceroos? Australia's biggest Liverpool fans face World Cup dilemma
item.description: Ahead of Australia's clash with Egypt, one question dominates at Kleames Dawod's barbershop: can you cheer against Mohamed Salah?

item.title: Why super funds are looking overseas for strong returns as ETF listings hit a record
item.description: Australian shares ended the day little changed, but there is plenty happening beneath the surface. AMP Chief Investment Officer Anna Shelley explains why many superannuation funds delivered double-digit returns last financial year, the role global markets including South Korea played, and whether recent volatility could reshape investment strategies. Plus, Rory Cunningham from the ASX unpacks the record boom in exchange traded funds and why more Australians are investing through ETFs, before Chris Weston from Pepperstone wraps up the day's market action and the forces driving the Australian sharemarket.

item.title: A controversial opener, crack at fifth title and Aussies to watch: Your Tour de France guide
item.description: The world's biggest cycling spectacle, the Tour de France returns on 4 July. This year's race could be more challenging than ever.

item.title: The Socceroos diehards putting on a festival in an unlikely location
item.description: The community of Jabal Mohsen has no football field of its own, but some here are going all out for the Socceroos.

item.title: Suspected bird flu case found in NSW, authorities say
item.description: This adds to the five known cases in two other states.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English | Online safety commissioner launches legal action against Elon Musk
item.description: A news bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Inside the 'extremely complex' operation to save a Venezuelan trapped for eight days
item.description: On the ground in Venezuela, rescuers describe to SBS News the harrowing effort to retrieve a man who, against all odds, remained alive eight days after twin earthquakes rocked Venezuela.

item.title: 'A miracle': Man pulled from rubble in earthquake-hit Venezuela
item.description: A security guard has pulled out from under the rubble in Venezuela, eight days after twin earthquakes struck the country. Seven countries were involved in the rescue effort as part of what has been described as a complex logistical operation.

item.title: Record car sales as ASX rallies
item.description: Australians are buying new cars at a record pace despite ongoing cost of living pressures, with June sales reaching an all-time monthly high and electric vehicles and utes dominating the market.

item.title: More than 25,000 Australians are new millionaires. They're part of a growing global group
item.description: Swiss bank UBS has found that global wealth has expanded at its fastest pace since 2017. Here's why.

item.title: Your super may have been boosted by an unlikely sharemarket — here's how
item.description: An overseas stock exchange may have boosted your super last year. Plus, falling petrol prices and the rising ETF market.

item.title: 'I don't agree': One Nation supporters don't understand monoculturalism
item.description: This electorate "screams One Nation", but there's one issue supporters are confused about.

item.title: Free-range chicken owners advised to keep hens inside as bird flu threat spreads
item.description: Australia's animal health emergency body says poultry producers may want to keep free-range birds indoors for the next two weeks.

item.title: Keiko Fujimori has won Peru's presidential race
item.description: Keiko Fujimori's win reaffirms Latin America's rightward shift, while her leftist opponent Roberto Sanchez says he won't recognise her government.

item.title: Millions mourn as Iran begins week-long funeral for slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei
item.description: Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is not just the country's late Supreme Leader. He is now a martyr.

item.title: Denis watched his ancestral lands be ravaged as a kid. It sparked a major change
item.description: Indigenous Protected Areas are a homegrown but relatively unheard-of conservation model, making waves in Australia and around the world.

item.title: The surprising factor shaping young Australians' family plans
item.description: New research finds most young Australians still want children, but a lack of trust in society is emerging as a surprising influence.

item.title: '50 Years of Deadly': What is NAIDOC Week, and what's behind this year's theme?
item.description: National NAIDOC Week celebrations are being held across Australia this week to celebrate and recognise the history, culture and achievements of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. This year's theme is 'Fifty Years of Deadly', celebrating more than five decades of NAIDOC Week. NAIDOC Week runs from the 5th to the 12th of July. The Committee members say the milestone offers a chance to honour those who built the NAIDOC movement, and consider what still needs to change.

item.title: One Nation came second in the Hunter electorate last election. What do voters there think now?
item.description: Support for One Nation has been on the rise in recent months. But in the electorate of Hunter - a Labor stronghold in New South Wales - the party was the runner up in last year's election, one of only two electorates where that happened. SBS News reporter Kerrin Thomas travelled to Hunter to speak to residents about how they're feeling about the party's latest moves.

item.title: After a stunning World Cup debut winning hearts, where to now for Cape Verde players?
item.description: The Blue Sharks' fairytale run in the 2026 FIFA World Cup was partly powered by the African nation's diaspora community.

item.title: Great Barrier Reef avoids 'in danger' listing - but what can be done to ensure its future?
item.description: Conservationists are calling for greater government action to protect the Great Barrier Reef, as a looming El Nino weather system threatens more coral bleaching. In a draft decision, the UNESCO World Heritage Committee has recommended against putting the reef on its list of sites in danger, while requesting greater ambition on climate change and efforts to improve water quality.

item.title: Why more Australians are turning to this $350 billion investment pool
item.description: The ETF market momentum is only expected to continue, as Aussies seek low-cost entry points to grow their wealth.

item.title: '50 Years of Deadly': Inside 2026 NAIDOC Week celebrations
item.description: This year's NAIDOC Week theme celebrates the history, culture and achievements of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.

item.title: The overlooked factor in Australia's migration debate that's keeping figures high
item.description: As debate rages within Australia about migration levels, experts say a key element is being ignored.

item.title: PM 'unequivocally' apologises for 'shag, marry, date' podcast comments
item.description: Anthony Albanese picked Kylie Minogue in a suggestive party game on comedian Nikki Osborne's show.

item.title: Thousands evacuated and spectators banned from Tour de France as wildfires burn through Europe
item.description: Thousands have been forced to evacuate in some areas as wildfires burn in parts of Europe. Spectators have also been banned from lining the Tour de France route ahead of the third stage of the iconic race. Authorities say record-breaking heat waves and scant rainfall have turned forests and fields into tinderboxes, with forecasters warning that more extreme conditions are on the way.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 6 July 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: 'I'm astounded': The visa fee that's nearly tripled in price
item.description: A large fee increase could lead to some migrants reconsidering Australia, with some saying it sends a "bad signal".

item.title: Social media platforms under fire again at Antisemitism Royal Commission
item.description: Social media giant, Meta, has appeared at the Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion, defending its record on stopping online abuse. E-Safety Commissioner, Julie Inman Grant, has slammed social media platforms for failing to monitor so-called "gore" content.

item.title: Why petrol prices aren't rising as fast as expected despite halving of fuel excise discount
item.description: Petrol prices are edging higher after the fuel excise discount was halved, but why haven't they jumped by the full amount?

item.title: How do Australia's 'great deals' with its neighbours compare to China?
item.description: As Albanese shores up deals in the Pacific, he heads to a China stronghold.

item.title: 'My dating life is essentially non-existent': Why young people are staying single for longer
item.description: With singledom on the rise, technology replacing interaction, and people drinking less, some younger Australians are having less sex than previous generations.

item.title: 'I would do anything': The boy searching for his mother in the ruins
item.description: Among the rubble of Venezuela's earthquakes, I found grief, anger and extraordinary resilience.

item.title: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body, clearing path for US-backed technocratic committee
item.description: The National Committee for the Administration of Gaza was created by the Trump-backed Board of Peace established in October 2025.

item.title: 'Glaring problem': China's missile test could be a troubling signal, analysts warn
item.description: Some say this week's launch signals something bigger than a one-off provocation.

item.title: 'Despicable': French striker Kylian Mbappé condemns Paraguayan senator as racist
item.description: Mbappé was responding to comments made by a Paraguayan senator on social media after the national team's loss to France.

item.title: INTERVIEW: NITV speaks to Richard Frankland about this year's Elder in Residence Oration
item.description: A WARNING THAT THIS INTERVIEW CONTAINS REFERENCES TO AN INDIGENOUS PERSON WHO HAS DIED Respected Aboriginal writer, activist and filmmaker Richard Frankland has delivered this year’s Elder in Residence Oration, an annual talk which invites reflection on First Nations issues. The Gunditjmara man has previously served in the Army and worked with the Royal Commission into Aboriginal Deaths in Custody. He is encouraging all Australians to keep truth telling, justice and unity front of mind this NAIDOC Week, telling NITV that truth-telling is essential for Australia's growth as a nation.

item.title: 'We should be concerned': Scientists warn onset of El Niño could wreak global havoc
item.description: From Europe to the Pacific, extreme weather events are devastating communities and wildlife across the globe as scientists warn of a strengthening El Niño . With the weather system predicted to rapidly emerge in the coming months, experts say no region is immune to the deepening crisis.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 7 July 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: What Australia can learn from Norway about creating athletes like Haaland
item.description: Norway does not encourage competition in youth sport. Is that approach breeding international sporting champions?

item.title: Authorities urge public not to panic as bird flu tally climbs
item.description: A further two suspected cases have been detected, while authorities across the country ask for calm.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese continues his diplomatic push through the Pacific
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has set an ambitious timeline for a comprehensive new treaty with the Solomon Islands, saying he'd like to see it completed by the end of this year. It's part of a diplomatic push across the region, following landmark deals with Fiji, PNG, Vanuatu and Indonesia. This comes amid rising concern over China's role in the region - underscored this week by military drills.

item.title: Ronaldo's farewell, Folarin Balogun's FIFA-red card controversy, US World Cup exit
item.description: Spain defeats Portugal 1-nil, as Cristiano Ronaldo played his last World Cup match. Belgium win 4-1 against host nation USA, which means all host nations, including Canada and Mexico are out of the FIFA World Cup 2026™ tournament. US President Donald Trump admits he personally intervened with FIFA over US player Folarin Balogun's previous red card suspension. Human rights group FairSquare calls for his intervention over this issue to be investigated as a breach of FIFA's rules on political neutrality. But FIFA President Gianni Infantino says the disciplinary committee is independent and he had no involvement in the decision. Join your host Haylena Krishnamoorthy for this episode of World Cup Daily | The 90+ Podcast, as she talks to sports law expert to discuss the red card controversy, and Article 27.

item.title: Microsoft slashes jobs as investors question global AI investment
item.description: In this episode of SBS On the Money, Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Piers Bolger, Chief Investment Officer at Infinity Asset Management, about the Australian sharemarket’s decline, the impact of Microsoft’s latest job cuts and whether global companies are overinvesting in AI.

item.title: Australia is courting its next Pacific partner
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has visited the Solomon Islands just as China launches a nuclear-capable ballistic missile in the region.

item.title: Hamas says it will step aside. Here's what it means for Gaza — and what obstacles remain
item.description: While the move could reshape who governs Gaza, experts say it does not resolve the core dispute preventing a lasting peace.

item.title: 'Shaped the lives of thousands': The teacher who never stopped showing up
item.description: The longest-serving Aboriginal Education Officer in NSW, Aunty Ruth Simms, has inspired generations in and out of the classroom.

item.title: Inside Narendra Modi's Australia visit: Optics are warm. Politics are harder
item.description: The applause, the ambition and the tensions shaping Narendra Modi's Australia visit.

item.title: IOC lifts ban on Russian athletes, paving the way for 2028 Games return
item.description: The war that prompted the suspension of the Russian Olympic Committee in the first place continues.

item.title: Some Triple Zero calls impacted, minister confirms, as Telstra says software glitch caused major outage
item.description: Telstra has been forced to carry out hundreds of welfare checks after an hours-long outage that disrupted everything from phone services to trains.

item.title: We had an almost sexless marriage for 30 years. One decision changed everything
item.description: Tess and Craig experienced a disconnect in their sex life for decades. They share how they overcame this together.

item.title: Backlash begins after IOC lifts ban on Russia ahead of 2028 LA Games
item.description: The International Olympic Committee has provisionally lifted its suspension of the Russian athletes. The decision marks a significant step towards Russia's reintegration into the Olympic fold ahead of the Los Angeles 2028 Games.

item.title: Who needs allies? NATO leaders do more on arms as Trump vows to do less
item.description: NATO leaders are meeting in Turkiye for a summit overshadowed by US President Donald Trump's demands to do less, while Europe does more. Following major Russian attacks on Ukraine and missile tests from China, European allies are under pressure to heed US demands and bolster their defences.

item.title: Telstra says services are coming back online | SBS News in Easy English 8 July 2026
item.description: A news bulletin for English language learners

item.title: The European country preparing for a Russian attack, as Putin weighs odds
item.description: Poland says the coming months are "critical", but experts say Russia is cruising for a bruising if it provokes war on two fronts.

item.title: As Telstra outage eases, questions remain
item.description: A major service outage at Telstra has sparked chaos across the nation, disrupting communications, transport and business payments. The Triple Zero Custodian is investigating a number of cases, although it appears back-up systems have largely proven effective. Meanwhile, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has continued his Pacific push, hosting a number of regional leaders.

item.title: Messi leads Argentina, Egypt furious, Switzerland win in penalty shootout, heartbreak for Colombia
item.description: Egyptian coach Hossam Hassan says they 'have suffered injustice', following VAR upset. He will not be watching any more games the FIFA World Cup 2026™ tournament. Lionel Messi inspires Argentina’s fightback, before Enzo Fernandez completed an incredible 3-2 comeback deep into stoppage time. Switzerland eliminated Colombia 4-3 on penalties to reach their first World Cup quarter-final since 1954. Ruben Vargas struck the winning penalty. Join your host Haylena Krishnamoorthy for this episode of World Cup Daily | The 90+ Podcast, as she breaks today’s results with SBS Spanish Executive Producer, Carlos Colina, ahead of the France vs Morocco match on Friday.

item.title: How could a mobile outage disrupt trains? An expert explains
item.description: Here are the underlying tech connections between a telecommunications network crash and rail service disruptions.

item.title: Telstra shares hit February low following network outage
item.description: The Australian sharemarket opened sharply lower as renewed hostilities in the Middle East rattled investors, before recovering most of its losses by the close. Energy stocks surged on a jump in oil prices, while Telstra slid after a network outage sent its shares to their lowest level since February. SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Francesco De Stradis from Ord Minnett about the day's market moves and what investors should watch next.

item.title: Trump says ceasefire agreement is 'over' as US launches renewed strikes on Iran
item.description: The United States has initiated a new wave of attacks in the Middle East for the second consecutive day.

item.title: How Norway's seemingly low-drama royals became engulfed in turmoil
item.description: A criminal trial, royal controversies and a changing public mood has put Norway’s monarchy in the spotlight.

item.title: Transport delays remain after Wednesday’s Telstra outage | Morning News Bulletin 9 July 2026
item.description: Telstra blames a software fault for Wednesday's nationwide outage; India's Prime Minister arrives in Melbourne for high-stakes talks with Anthony Albanese; the stage is set for the World Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: INTERVIEW: What does Australia's latest health report reveal ?
item.description: The Australian Institute of Health and Welfare has released its 2026 health report, revealing the key drivers of health outcomes across the country, and where targeted attention is most needed. Researchers say Australians born today are expected to live longer than previous generations, cancer survival rates are improving, and coronary heart disease deaths are decreasing. But head of the Institute's Flagship Reporting Unit Melanie Dunford says the findings also show Australia is facing some significant health challenges and persistent inequalities

item.title: INTERVIEW: SBS Hindi speaks to India's High Commissioner in Australia about the visit of Narendra Modi
item.description: The Indian prime minister Narendra Modi is in Australia for three days. The visit is expected to feature more conversations about two-way trade and tertiary education deals, as well as a deal to unleash swathes of Australian uranium into India. Ahead of Mr Modi's arrival, SBS Hindi’s Natasha Kaul spoke to Indian High Commissioner to Australia, Nagesh Singh, about what to expect from the visit.

item.title: 'Dead pigs': The coded language inside a 'ruthless' network of online sexual predators in Germany
item.description: A Berlin court has convicted a Chinese man over his alleged involvement in an online network of sexual predators bragging and planning attacks on Telegram. The Telegram networks uncovered by German investigators are understood to have thousands of participants in a case echoing that of Gisele Pelicot in France.

item.title: Prime Minister Narendra Modi in Australia | Midday News Bulletin 8 July 2026
item.description: Australia's Indian diaspora gathering in Melbourne to see Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Mexico threatens to sue over ICE's fatal shooting of a citizen in the US, England wildcard Arthur Fery makes history at Wimbledon.

item.title: India's Prime Minister visits Australia | SBS News in Easy English 8 July 2026
item.description: A news bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Australian milk, Indian tea: Narendra Modi talks up Australian relationship in stadium address
item.description: There were more empty seats than at Modi's last stadium address in 2023, but there was no less enthusiasm among the crowd of Indian Australians.

item.title: Angus Taylor defends Liberal senator's Triple Zero test calls during Telstra outage
item.description: Sarah Henderson's emergency service test is under scrutiny as Telstra says customers can now "feel confident" calling Triple Zero.

item.title: Trump may have offered Ukraine exactly what it wants. But there's a major catch
item.description: Donald Trump has offered to let Ukraine produce high-powered missiles — but what is the US president trying to achieve from the deal?

item.title: Final eight: Every team playing in the World Cup quarter-finals
item.description: From 48 teams down to just eight, here's your guide to who's playing in the 2026 FIFA World Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: Australia and India move closer to a uranium export deal | Evening News Bulletin 9 July 2026
item.description: Telstra under fire over the Triple Zero outage, Australia and India move closer to a uranium export deal, FIFA’s president faces an ethics complaint over Donald Trump’s World Cup intervention.

item.title: Trump says the Iran deal is over, an angry NATO lovefest and has Hamas given up politics?
item.description: Donald Trump calls the Iranian regime ‘scum’ as the US and Iran trade strikes. What's next for Iran's leadership as the country gives Ayatollah Ali Khamenei a long goodbye. Hamas agrees to dissolves its government, but will that change things? Also, Trump gives his NATO allies a kicking and a hug as Ukraine gets the nod to produce Patriot missiles. Plus, mystery balls plague another Australian beach.

item.title: War jitters hit the market as Australia’s biggest IPO of the year makes a strong debut
item.description: The Australian sharemarket fell for a fourth straight session as escalating conflict in the Middle East pushed oil prices higher and weighed on investor sentiment, while energy stocks outperformed and Telstra gained ground. SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Nick Schoenmaker from Portfolio Construction Management about the day’s market moves, and Nick Schoenmaker also explains what the strong debut of construction group FDC Consolidated, Australia’s biggest IPO of the year, could mean for the future pipeline of sharemarket listings.

item.title: ABC and SBS questioned by Royal Commission on Antisemitism
item.description: The ABC and SBS have defended their reporting on conflict in the Middle East, after facing scrutiny at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion. Australia’s Special Envoy to Combat Antisemitism Jillian Segal also spoke to the inquiry, calling for both broadcasters to establish an independent oversight body. ABC and SBS maintain their reporting and editorial approaches have been accurate, balanced and impartial.

item.title: Uranium, universities, and 'top-tier' security: Inside the new Albanese-Modi agreements
item.description: Rivals in cricket, but allies in defence. Here's what the two nations have committed to.

item.title: Iran strikes military sites in Gulf states after top negotiator's warning against US 'bullying'
item.description: The attacks on Kuwait, Qatar and Bahrain followed recent US strikes on Iran's southern coastal and eastern provinces.

item.title: Researchers discover new clue that helps explain severe COVID-19
item.description: New research has uncovered a previously unknown route the virus can use inside the body.

item.title: Fanfare and protests as Modi mania comes to Melbourne | Morning News Bulletin 10 July 2026
item.description: In this bulletin, thousands turn out to see Narendra Modi at a Melbourne stadium; Andy Burnham on track to become the UK's next Prime Minister and in World Cup news, an all-Argentinian officiating team appointed for the France and Morocco quarter final.

item.title: AI fuelling online sexual harm for Australian teenagers, study finds
item.description: At least one young person in every Year 12 classroom has experienced or known someone who has been the target of AI-facilitated sexual abuse. That's the shocking finding from new national research that is the first national estimate of how often AI is involved in the online sexual victimisation of Australian children - and how AI has changed both how young people are harmed and how they seek help. Advocates are urging governments and stakeholders to catch up with evolving technology

item.title: Body of slain Iranian supreme leader arrives at Shia shrine as crowds chant 'Kill Trump'
item.description: Renewed US-Iran attacks on shipping and global supplies could stress the global economy yet again.

item.title: Body of slain Iranian supreme leader arrives at Shia shrine as crowds chant 'Kill Trump'
item.description: Renewed US-Iran attacks on shipping and global supplies could stress the global economy yet again.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Controversial UK politician Nigel Farage forces by-election over financial allegations
item.description: One of Britain's most controversial politicians and leader of the right-wing Reform Party, Nigel Farage, has announced he is quitting his seat so he can clear his name over financial allegations. His resignation has triggered a by-election in the Essex seat of Clacton - but none of the major parties plan to stand against him. At this stage, there is only one satirical opponent standing - Count Binface, who always appears in public with a dustbin on his head. Mr Farage was a key figure in the campaign for Britain to leave the EU. A donation of five million pounds by a supporter based in Thailand has seen accusations that Mr Farage should have declared it to Parliament, but didn't. He claims the establishment and the media are using “foul means” to stop him. Tim Bale is professor of politics at Queen Mary University of London and he told the Associated Press Mr Farage is taking quite a gamble.

item.title: WATCH: Mbappé leads Golden Boot race with stunner against Morocco in quarter-final victory
item.description: The French captain struck again to secure his team's place in the semi-finals.

item.title: 'Not your enemy': Pauline Hanson and Angus Taylor's stoush amid preference question
item.description: Angus Taylor has doubled down on his argument that One Nation policies are "incoherent".

item.title: Australia signs new defence, nuclear deals with India during controversial visit
item.description: India and Australia have announced a series of new nuclear, security and defence deals during Narendra Modi's visit to Australia. His first visit in three years has had a mixed reception, from a stadium filled with supporters, to protesters rallying against his government's human rights record.

item.title: Telstra chiefs back online to face outage backlash | Midday News Bulletin 10 July 2026
item.description: Fears for the ceasefire as Iran and the US trade strikes, Telstra's CEO apologises for Wednesday's chaotic nationwide outage, France advances to the final four in the World Cup after defeating Morocco in the quarter final.

item.title: First case of bird flu detected in local wildlife
item.description: Agriculture Minister Julie Collins has confirmed the first domestic case of H5 bird flu that has not come from a migratory bird.

item.title: As Iran and US trade strikes, what comes next for the Middle East?
item.description: There's been a further exchange of strikes between the US and Iran, putting the three-week old ceasefire deal under fresh strain. The latest strikes comes at the end of mass funeral commemorations for slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in the initial US strikes.

item.title: Veteran broadcaster and former senator Derryn Hinch dies aged 82
item.description: Hinch's long career spanned newspapers, radio and television.

item.title: 'Lot of questions' for Telstra; death found to be unrelated to Triple Zero outage
item.description: Telstra has rejected claims that Wednesday's mass outage was related to its job cuts.

item.title: Police investigating death after Telstra outage | SBS News in Easy English 10 July 2026
item.description: A bulletin for English language learners

item.title: India to host historic first-ever foreign league match with first Big Bash clash in December
item.description: It's a one-year arrangement for now.

item.title: Neil the Seal's reign of terror is over. But he'll be back
item.description: Neil's cheeky antics have made him an internet sensation, but the elephant seal's annual visit is part of a vital survival process.

item.title: Bird flu spreads to Australian wildlife | Evening News Bulletin 10 July 2026
item.description: Bird flu spreads to Australian wildlife, The body of a missing 13-year-old girl is found in western Victoria, Bronson Xerri quits the Bulldogs for Melbourne.

item.title: Andy Burham set to become British PM: but can Count Binface dispose of Nigel Farage?
item.description: Andy Burnham has taken another leap toward replacing Keir Starmer as UK Prime Minister, after securing 322 nominations from his party. Reform UK's Nigel Farage has also dramatically announced he will resign as a member of parliament, forcing a snap by-election. The populist leader's only confirmed rival in the seat of Clacton is a comedian who goes by the name 'Count Binface'.

item.title: If the US-Iran war reignites, how badly could Australia be impacted?
item.description: What could it mean for Australia if the US-Iran ceasefire continues to unravel and fighting intensifies once again?

item.title: World Cup: France ends Morocco's dream as Mbappé sends Les Bleus through
item.description: France ends Morocco's dream in the FIFA World Cup 2026™ tournament. Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé scored the goals for their side, sending France into World Cup semi-finals. French coach Didier Deschamps says he's proud of Mbappé's extraordinary performance. History for the stirker with 20 goals at a World Cup and his eighth of this tournament, drawing him level with Lionel Messi in the race for the Golden Boot. Join your host Haylena Krishnamoorthy for this episode of World Cup Daily | The 90+ Podcast, as she talks to former Matilda legend and SBS World Cup analyst, Lydia William to break down today's result. What's the psychology behind each penalty? Is Morocco a blueprint for African nations? The Morocco - France match comes with history - but why? What can we expect to see in the matches ahead?

item.title: Westpac brings forward rate cut forecast as ASX snaps losing streak.
item.description: Ricardo Gonçalves examines why Westpac Chief Economist Luci Ellis now expects the RBA to begin cutting interest rates in 2027 instead of 2028, while still forecasting two more rate rises before then. Plus, the Australian sharemarket ends a four-day losing streak, with miners leading the gains as gold and uranium stocks rally, while Telstra slips further following its recent network outage. Dianne Colledge from Morgans also joins the podcast to break down the day's market moves and discusses the latest wave of US IPOs.

item.title: ‘I know nothing’: Bradley John Murdoch’s last interview with police released publicly
item.description: NT Police made two separate attempts to convince Bradley Murdoch to reveal the whereabouts of Peter Falconio's remains.

item.title: US-Iran ceasefire "over" but Trump says talks to resume | Morning News Bulletin 11 July 2026
item.description: Talks to resume between US and Iran but Trump reiterates the ceasefire is over; a 16-year-old boy is arrested over the death of a Victorian teenage girl; Spain and Belgium face off in World Cup quarter final

item.title: Rate cuts could come earlier than expected — but there's a catch
item.description: One of Australia's leading banks says rate cuts could come sooner than expected. Plus: petrol prices, a big ASX IPO and a Telstra outage.

item.title: 'You're not anybody's friend': In Jude's line of work, you can be 'disappeared'
item.description: The Pacific tuna fishery supplies more than half of the world's catch. But its abundance has made it a target for illegal fishing.

item.title: Tuna Wars: Cracking down on illegal fishing
INTERVIEW: AI is escalating sexual child abuse online
item.description: A new national study has found that artificial intelligence (AI) escalates the amount of online sexual exploitation against children. The research, a collaboration between the International Centre for Missing and Exploited Children Australia and the Australian Federal Police’s Centre to Counter Child Exploitation, found that at least one young person in every Year 12 classroom has experienced or known someone who has experienced AI-facilitated sexual abuse. Associate Professor Tim Cubitt from Adelaide University, an author of the study, tells SBS reporter Wing Kuang that AI is not only escalating sexual child abuse online, it’s also become a safehaven that many children who experienced sexual exploitation turn to for help.

item.title: Spain heads to the World Cup semi-finals after fiery 2-1 thriller against Belgium
item.description: Despite Spain's dominant attempts at possession, Belgium put up a fight throughout the match to thrill travelling supporters.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Tuna Wars: Allan Rahari talks about the fishery's major threats
item.description: Allan Rahari, the Interim Deputy Director General of the Pacific Islands Forum Fisheries Agency and the Director of Fisheries Operations. He calls tuna the "backbone" of the Pacific and speaks with SBS News about a major threat to the fishery: illegal, unreported and unregulated fishing.

item.title: United States agrees to more talks with Iran, but insists truce is over
item.description: Donald Trump said he has agreed to further negotiations with Iran, adding that the ceasefire is definitely done.

item.title: Man arrested on suspicion of murdering ex-UK minister Ann Widdecombe
item.description: Police say that there is no evidence that the death of Widdecombe, 78, a well-known conservative figure in the UK, was politically motivated.

item.title: The 'invisible migrants' no-one talked about — until now
item.description: The untold stories of Dutch migrants who made Australia home after World War Two.

item.title: War in Iran prevents verification of Iran's declared nuclear facilities | Midday News Bulletin 11 July 2026
item.description: Atomic Energy Agency says it's lost knowledge of Iran's nuclear facilities; the 25th anniversary of the backpacker murder approaches; Spain is headed to the World Cup semi-finals after beating Belgium.

item.title: 'Why not?': Norwegian fans in Australia dare to dream after historic World Cup run
item.description: Australia's Norwegian community has embraced their national team's fairytale World Cup campaign.

item.title: News story saying Labor minister called One Nation voters 'unintelligent' shown to be fake
item.description: A fake SBS News article about Labor minister Clare O'Neil is being shared on social media. She never made the comments.

item.title: Australia calls for de-escalation in Middle East | Evening News Bulletin 11 July 2026
item.description: Australia calls for de-escalation in the Middle East; India and New Zealand elevate bilateral relations; Spain beats Belgium to book their spot in the World Cup semifinals.

item.title: World Cup: Merino strikes late, Spain beat Belgium to reach semi-finals against France
item.description: Spain is through to the FIFA World Cup 2026™ tournament semi-finals after a dramatic 2-1 win over Belgium. Fabián Ruiz opened the scoring for the Spaniards, before Belgium equalised through Charles De Ketelaere. But substitute Mikel Merino - also the hero in the previous round against Portugal - came off the bench in the 86th minute, and scored just before stoppage time. Lamine Yamal was named Player of the Match after another electric performance. Join your host Haylena Krishnamoorthy for this episode of World Cup Daily | The 90+ Podcast, as she talks to former Matildas legend and SBS World Cup pundit, Sarah Walsh to break down the match.

item.title: Hobart's MONA museum plans Bangkok expansion
item.description: The Premier of Tasmania said he hoped the project would strengthen Tasmania's ties with Thailand.

item.title: Nearly two million evacuated as Typhoon Bavi barrels towards China
item.description: Two landslides triggered by heavy rains driven have killed 17 people in the Philippines.

item.title: Iranian Foreign Minister in Oman for talks on Strait of Hormuz | Morning News Bulletin 12 July 2026
item.description: Talks in Oman about arrangements for the Strait of Hormuz; Telstra confirms its CEO will appear before a Senate inquiry; the NRL plans a party to celebrate the birthday of Jai Arrow, who is battling motor neurone disease.

item.title: Anthony's city apartment costs the same as the Brisbane sharehouse he rented 20 years ago
item.description: Vienna has spent decades investing in a social housing system that supports half its residents. Could Australia replicate it?

item.title: Redrawn maps, proof of citizenship and ICE agents: How voting in the US is set to change
item.description: Amid Donald Trump's sweeping efforts to change voting rules, the midterms could have lasting consequences for US elections.

item.title: Central Australia's buffel grass problem
item.description: Record rainfall in Australia's outback this year has transformed a usually red desert landscape into a green oasis. The sight can even be seen from space. And while it's welcomed by some - environmentalists and Traditional Owners say, in this instance, green is not good.

item.title: INTERVIEW: SBS Commentator assesses the FIFA World Cup so far
item.description: The 2026 Fifa World Cup has finally reached the quarter final stage of the tournament. It's been a gruelling ride for our SBS commentators, including former Socceroo Tommy Oar. As we head into the business end of the competition, Tommy looks back on the story so far and what may lay ahead, He's talking to Chris Tan

item.title: Tuna Wars: one of the world's most dangerous jobs
item.description: This is the second part of our special series about tuna fishing in the Pacific. According to fisheries officials in the Pacific, one of the most difficult to pin down breaches when it comes to overfishing is the ability to monitor catch - and ensure compliance with fishing licences. In an effort to combat this - fisheries observers are stationed on many commercial fishing vessels to keep watch - but it is also considered one of the most dangerous jobs in the world. And this story was supported by the Pulitzer Centre.

item.title: South Africa World Cup midfielder Jayden Adams dies at the age of 25
item.description: Jayden Adams played in three matches at the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: WATCH: Argentina march on to the World Cup semi-finals as Switzerland see red
item.description: Meanwhile, England ended Norway's World Cup dream after Jude Bellingham put in a heroic shift.

item.title: US congressman says he was detained by Israeli settlers in occupied West Bank
item.description: Ro Khanna says he was confronted and detained by Israeli settlers brandishing US-made assault rifles.

item.title: Venezuelans facing 'the same uncertainty' six months after Maduro's abduction by the US
item.description: The economic situation in Venezuela was already one of the worst in the world before the US intervention.

item.title: US resumes strikes after Iran fires on ship in Strait of Hormuz | Midday News Bulletin 12 July 2026
item.description: US resumes strikes after Iran fires on ship in Strait of Hormuz; Venezuela seeks release of assets held abroad to fund post-earthquake reconstruction; England through to the semi-finals in the FIFA football World Cup

item.title: Iran strikes Gulf neighbours in retaliation for US attacks, says it's hit two ships in Hormuz
item.description: Donald Trump has declared the end of a ceasefire meant to halt the war started by the US and Israel on 28 February.

item.title: What we know about the 2030 men's FIFA World Cup
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has reached the pointy end, but planning for the next tournament is already well underway.

item.title: Fresh US strikes on Iran | Evening News Bulletin 12 July 2026
item.description: Fresh US strikes on Iranian military targets; police lay charges against a man who allegedly trespassed to film the hideout of fugitive Dezi Freeman; Argentina is through to the FIFA World Cup semi-finals.

item.title: Queensland woman's sister shares heartfelt message after ex-partner charged with murder
item.description: On Saturday night, a group of wildlife hunters found what police believe to be the remains of 30-year-old Jana Armstrong.

item.title: World Cup: Bellingham stars as England beats Norway; VAR chaos; Argentina reach semis; Embolo red card
item.description: England and Argentina to meet in semi-final. Norway and Switzerland out of the FIFA World Cup 2026™ tournament. England win after a 2-1 extra-time win against Norway, double from Jude Bellingham. Argentina beat Switzerland 3-1 in extra-time. A couple of controversial VAR reviews in both quarter final matches. A red card for Switzerland's Breel Embolo, who became the first player sent off under FIFA's new mistaken identity law, after a VAR review. Erling Haaland leaves the tournament with pride for the next generation. Join your host Haylena Krishnamoorthy for this episode of World Cup Daily | The 90+ Podcast, as she talks to former Central Coast Mariners coach and Sydney Football Club assistant Phil Moss, to break down the England-Argentina match.
